name: If Workflow
description: Automated workflow: If Workflow. This workflow integrates 16 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, code, scheduleTrigger, agent. It contains 26 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Agent Classification

options.systemMessage:
=You are a classification assistant. 

Your role is to analyze the title and description of an article and determine if it is related to sustainability. 

You must only return {"answer": true} if the article is clearly related to sustainability (e.g., environmental protection, renewable energy, sustainable development, climate action, green economy, etc.). 

If it is not clearly related, return {"answer": false}.

If the description is empty or missing, rely only on the title. Your response must be only one of the two JSON options: {"answer": true} or {"answer": false}. Do not provide explanations.

parameters.subject: Your Sustainability News Digest from LogiGreen

nodes.notes: This gmail node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Generate Email HTML

parameters.jsCode:
const summary = `Welcome to the EU Sustainability News Digest provided by LogiGreen Consulting.`; const articles = items.map(item => item.json); // each item is an article let html = `

🌍 EU News Digest – ${new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-GB', { day: 'numeric', month: 'long', year: 'numeric' })}

${summary}

`; for (const article of articles) { const link = article.link.startsWith("http") ? article.link : `{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}{article.link}`; html += `
${article.image ? `` : ''}

${article.type} | ${article.date}

${article.title}

${article.description || ''}

${article.read_time ? `

${article.read_time}

` : ''}
`; } html += `

You received this email as part of the EU Sustainability News Digest project.

`; return [{ json: { email_body: html } }];

### 1. Workflow Trigger with Cron Job
The workflow is triggered every morning at 08:30 am (local time)

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
### 4. Generate HTML page and send by email
This block collects all the articles of the day to create a prettified HTML page that is sent using the Gmail node.
#### How to setup?
- **Gmail Node:** set up your Gmail API credentials
[Learn more about the Gmail Trigger Node]

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
### 2. Scrapping and Parsing of Articles blocks
This starts with the HTTP node collecting HTML code that is parsed to extract Article Titles, Link, Image Cover and Reading time.

#### How to setup?
*Nothing to do*

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
### 3. Classifiy all the articles (Sustainability: true or false)
This starts with the If node that filters based on the scope date fixed by you. Through the loop, the AI Agent classify the articles using the title and description.
The ones that are flagged as "sustainability" are recorded in a Google Sheet.

#### How to setup?
- **Record results in the Google Sheet Node**:
   1. Add your Google Sheet API credentials to access the Google Sheet file
   2. Select the file using the list, an URL or an ID
   3. Select the sheet in which you want to record the articles
   4. Map the fields: **sustainability, type, date, title, link, description, image, read time**
  [Learn more about the Google Sheet Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
- **AI Agent with the Chat Model**:
   1. Add a chat model with the required credentials *(Example: Open AI 4o-mini)*

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

If it is not clearly related, return {"answer": false}.

If the description is empty or missing, rely only on the title. Your response must be only one of the two JSON options: {"answer": true} or {"answer": false}. Do not provide explanations.

parameters.subject: Your Sustainability News Digest from LogiGreen

parameters.content:
### 4. Generate HTML page and send by email
This block collects all the articles of the day to create a prettified HTML page that is sent using the Gmail node.
#### How to setup?
- **Gmail Node:** set up your Gmail API credentials
[Learn more about the Gmail Trigger Node]

### 1. Workflow Trigger with Cron Job
The workflow is triggered every morning at 08:30 am (local time)

#### How to setup?
- Select the time you want to set it up

### 2. Scrapping and Parsing of Articles blocks
This starts with the HTTP node collecting HTML code that is parsed to extract Article Titles, Link, Image Cover and Reading time.

#### How to setup?
*Nothing to do*

### 3. Classifiy all the articles (Sustainability: true or false)
This starts with the If node that filters based on the scope date fixed by you. Through the loop, the AI Agent classify the articles using the title and description.
The ones that are flagged as "sustainability" are recorded in a Google Sheet.

#### How to setup?
- **Record results in the Google Sheet Node**:
   1. Add your Google Sheet API credentials to access the Google Sheet file
   2. Select the file using the list, an URL or an ID
   3. Select the sheet in which you want to record the articles
   4. Map the fields: **sustainability, type, date, title, link, description, image, read time**
  [Learn more about the Google Sheet Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
- **AI Agent with the Chat Model**:
   1. Add a chat model with the required credentials *(Example: Open AI 4o-mini)*

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Trigger at 08:30 am".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "If Node" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model3" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Agent Classification" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Sustainability News" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Send to your mailing list" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Parse Article Blocks" node (n8n-nodes-base.html) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Extract Articles Blocks" node (n8n-nodes-base.html) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Query EU News Website" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Out by Article Block" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Loop Over Articles" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge Article + Flag" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Record Results" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Structured Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Sustainability Flag".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Incoming triggers will qualified/formatted, new rows will be written to Google Sheets, and email replies or alerts will be sent automatically.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
